title: Indentation and Blocks
summary: Understand why indentation controls structure in Python.

sketchBlocks.title: Understanding Indentation
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
In Python, indentation is not just for readability; it defines the structure of your code. For example:

```python
if condition:
    # This block is executed if the condition is true
    print('Condition is true')
else:
    # This block is executed if the condition is false
    print('Condition is false')
```

In this example, the indented lines under `if` and `else` are part of their respective blocks. If you forget to indent, Python will raise an `IndentationError`. 

**Your turn:** Try writing your own `if` statement with an `else` block!

Line by line: read the first statement to see which value is stored or checked, then follow each conditional branch in order to see which path prints the final result.

sketchBlocks.title: Common Indentation Errors
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
Indentation errors can be frustrating. For instance, if you mix tabs and spaces, Python will not know how to interpret your code. Here's an example of a common mistake:

```python
if condition:
print('This will cause an error')
```

In this case, the print statement should be indented. Always use either spaces or tabs consistently throughout your code.

**Try this:** Fix the indentation in the example above.

Line by line: read the first statement to see which value is stored or checked, then follow each conditional branch in order to see which path prints the final result.

quizDraft.title: What does indentation control in Python?
quizDraft.prompt: What is the primary purpose of indentation in Python?
options: To make the code look nice
To define the structure of the code
quizDraft.hint: Think about how Python interprets your code.

help.concept: Indentation in Python is crucial as it defines the blocks of code that belong together, such as those under an if statement.
help.hint_2: Consider how other programming languages handle code blocks.

quizDraft.title: Identify Indentation Errors
quizDraft.prompt: Select all the code snippets that contain indentation errors.
options:
if x > 5:
print('x is greater than 5')

help.concept: Indentation errors occur when the code blocks are not properly aligned.
help.hint_1: Check for lines that should be indented but aren't.
help.hint_2: Remember that all lines in a block must have the same indentation level.
